rulings.Case Title: JANET WHITAKER et al VS. BORG-WARNER CORPORATION BY ITS SUCCESSOR IN et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, July 9, 2015 in Department 503, Line 1. Defendant Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its initial burden. Plaintiffs failed to present evidence to create a triable issue whether Mr. Whitaker was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. The Court did not need to consider the deposition testimony of George Kirk to find that defendant sustained its initial burden. Defendant's objections to plaintiffs' responses to interrogatories (Exhibit C) are sustained and its objections to its standard interrogatory responses (Exhibits E, H) are overruled. Defendant's objections nos. 4, 5, 6 and 7 are also overruled. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: NORMA MURRAY, AS SUCCESSOR IN INTEREST TO et al VS. AMCORD,INC et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, July 9, 2015 in Department 503, Line 2. Defendant Parex USA, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden under either prong of Aguilar. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Murray was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Plaintiffs' responses to defendant's special interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Defendant also failed to sustain its burden of negating an essential element of plaintiffs' claims. Plaintiffs' objections to paragraph 12 of Tod Kingsland's declaration are sustained and their objections to the Paul Lagier deposition testimony submitted with defendant's reply are sustained. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: LAWRENCE GRAUBART ET AL VS. AMERICAN CYANAMID COMNPANY AKA WYETH HOLDINGS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION As To Defendant Cyprus Amax Minerals Company
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, July 9, 2015 in Department 503, Line 3. Plaintiffs' motion for summary adjudication as to defendant Cyprus Amax Minerals Company is continued to July 16, 2015 per plaintiffs' amended notice filed June 5, 2015. = (503/TLJ)
